title: 6 Ways Sandboxes Prepare Your Agentforce for the Real World
author: Katie Muse

sections.heading: Introduction
sections.text: Between ‌rising customer expectations, skill shortages, and growing pressures for AI adoption — keeping up is a challenge. EnterAgentforce. We’re now in thethird wave of AI, where intelligent agent networks are reshaping the workplace. Agentforce unlocks a new level of productivity, powering “limitless digital labor.” From offering support 24/7 to seamless customer interactions, AI agents aretransformingevery part of an organization. But without proper Agentforce testing, even the most advanced AI can create challenges instead of solutions.

sections.heading: Why testing is essential to your Agentforce strategy
sections.text: Even groundbreaking innovations like Agentforce must be tested before deployment. Unlike traditional apps,AI agentsare non-deterministic – their behavior can vary between interactions. That’s why every step of the Agentforce testing and deployment cycle matters. From brainstorming and setup to thorough testing, deployment, and ongoing monitoring, skipping a single step can lead to real-world errors that impact your end users. It’s a crucial step to make sure your AI agent enhances workflows instead of creating obstacles, draining resources, and‌ damaging trust. A well-tested agent is an asset – not a liability. Take recruiting, for example. AnAgentforce-powered assistantcould autonomously handle administrative tasks, freeing recruiters to focus on building relationships with candidates. Using predefined criteria like skills, experience, and location, it could scan resumes, generate a curated shortlist, and highlight top candidates – eliminating time-consuming manual screening. But before deployment, it must be tested to ensure accurate data processing, regulatory compliance, and unbiased decision-making. Without thesesafeguards, even the most powerful AI can introduce risk instead of results.

sections.heading: 6 reasons why Agentforce testing in a Sandbox is key
sections.text: Salesforce Sandboxesprovide admins and developers with a secure environment to experiment and innovate. By replicating the production environment, teams can build, test, and refine new features, code, and integrations —without affecting live data and operations. Experiment with and refine Agentforce capabilities in an isolated environment to ensure smooth integration and deployment. Validate Agentforce workflows using live CRM andData 360data for real-world accuracy. With Data 360 unifying company-wide data, Sandboxes allow precise testing in acontrolled environment– delivering results that closely mirror production. Using a tool likeData Mask & Seed, you can effortlessly populate Sandboxes with fresh data and ensure sensitive information isn’t exposed. Simulate user interactions in a Sandbox to uncover patterns, refine AI responses, and improve natural language understanding. This ensures the AI adapts to common phrases and user behaviors before deployment.AI systems can struggle under heavy loads, causing delays or failures. Sandbox testing can identify bottlenecks. Using a scalability tool, LikeScale Test, your teams can test new features at a production environment’sfull traffic loadto make sure they are performant and resilient. Imagine an AI system accidentally exposing sensitive user data orviolating privacy regulations. TheAgentforce Trust Layerupholds data privacy and security across all interactions. Agentforce’s built-in guardrails protect data privacy, security, and accuracy. Testing in a Sandbox validates that these measures work correctly so your organization canconfidently deploy secure and compliant AI agents.Organizations often have unique challenges, such as preventing data loss or monitoring access.Agentforce 360 Platformaddresses these needs with solutions like real-time data controls and compliance tools. Safely test utterances and conversational scenarios to improve Agentforce’s understanding and response. TheAgentforce Testing Centerprovides a controlled space to simulate real-world interactions. For example, developers can test a range of customer queries and refine how the AI handles edge cases, ensuring reduced misunderstandings and errors when it goes live.Sometimes, AI can glitch in unexpected ways. Testing helps find unexpected behaviors and quirks that could disrupt user experience or even cause harm. Verify that AI agents correctly interpret and respond to specific topics by testing integrations and configurations. The more you test, the better your agents will understand their skill sets and take more precise actions. Repeated testing with iterations will help confirm accuracy and relevance, boosting performance during real-world interactions.

confidently deploy secure and compliant AI agents

sections.heading: Enhance your testing with data masking and seeding
sections.text: While Sandboxes make Agentforce testing less risky, you can take it a step further by quickly populating your sandbox with realistic data using data seeding, especially when a mid-cycle refresh isn’t feasible. Data masking, on the other hand, is crucial for maintaining data privacy and security. It allows you to use real data for testing without exposing sensitive information. With data masking, you can ensure that while you’re refining your agents, they don’t inadvertently misuse sensitive business or customer PII data. For any organization working with sensitive data, this step is especially important for maintaining compliance and mitigating the risk of data breaches or leaks. It’s even more critical for industries with strict regulatory requirements, such as healthcare and finance. By masking sensitive data, you can simulate real-world scenarios while ensuring compliance and protecting user information.

sections.heading: Prepare your Agentforce for deployment
sections.text: Testing lays the groundwork for creating a reliable, high-performing Agentforce that meets expectations immediately. By testing in a Sandbox with realistic, secure data, you can fine-tune your Agentforce in a controlled environment, ensuring seamless performance so you can be sure they’re ready for deployment. Learn more about risk-free Agentforce testing withSalesforce SandboxesandData Mask & Seed.

raw_html:
Between ‌rising customer expectations, skill shortages, and growing pressures for AI adoption — keeping up is a challenge. Enter Agentforce. 

We’re now in the third wave of AI, where intelligent agent networks are reshaping the workplace. Agentforce unlocks a new level of productivity, powering “limitless digital labor.” From offering support 24/7 to seamless customer interactions, AI agents are transforming every part of an organization. But without proper Agentforce testing, even the most advanced AI can create challenges instead of solutions.

Ready, set, Agentforce

Learn how Agentforce fits your business goals, launch your first agent, and build a plan for AI success on Trailhead, Salesforce’s free online learning platform.

Start learning
+1,500 points
Trail

Get Ready for Agentforce

Learn the ways of this trail.

Why testing is essential to your Agentforce strategy

Even groundbreaking innovations like Agentforce must be tested before deployment. Unlike traditional apps, AI agents are non-deterministic – their behavior can vary between interactions.      

That’s why every step of the Agentforce testing and deployment cycle matters. From brainstorming and setup to thorough testing, deployment, and ongoing monitoring, skipping a single step can lead to real-world errors that impact your end users. It’s a crucial step to make sure your AI agent enhances workflows instead of creating obstacles, draining resources, and‌ damaging trust. A well-tested agent is an asset – not a liability.               

Take recruiting, for example. An Agentforce-powered assistant could autonomously handle administrative tasks, freeing recruiters to focus on building relationships with candidates. Using predefined criteria like skills, experience, and location, it could scan resumes, generate a curated shortlist, and highlight top candidates – eliminating time-consuming manual screening. 

But before deployment, it must be tested to ensure accurate data processing, regulatory compliance, and unbiased decision-making. Without these safeguards, even the most powerful AI can introduce risk instead of results.        

D-I-Why? Deploy AI agents faster with Agentforce

Building and deploying autonomous AI agents takes time. Agentforce, the agentic layer of the Salesforce platform, can reduce time to market by 16x compared to DIY approaches — with 70% greater accuracy, according to a new Valoir report.

Read the report

6 reasons why Agentforce testing in a Sandbox is key

Salesforce Sandboxes provide admins and developers with a secure environment to experiment and innovate. By replicating the production environment, teams can build, test, and refine new features, code, and integrations — without affecting live data and operations. 

1. Build and refine without business disruption

Experiment with and refine Agentforce capabilities in an isolated environment to ensure smooth integration and deployment.    

2. Test real workflows with real data

Validate Agentforce workflows using live CRM and Data 360 data for real-world accuracy. With Data 360 unifying company-wide data, Sandboxes allow precise testing in a controlled environment – delivering results that closely mirror production. Using a tool like Data Mask & Seed, you can effortlessly populate Sandboxes with fresh data and ensure sensitive information isn’t exposed.

3. Monitor and analyze user behavior

Simulate user interactions in a Sandbox to uncover patterns, refine AI responses, and improve natural language understanding.  This ensures the AI adapts to common phrases and user behaviors before deployment.

AI systems can struggle under heavy loads, causing delays or failures. Sandbox testing can identify bottlenecks. Using a scalability tool, Like Scale Test, your teams can test new features at a production environment’s full traffic load to make sure they are performant and resilient.

4. Validate the Trust Layer across interactions

Imagine an AI system accidentally exposing sensitive user data or violating privacy regulations. The Agentforce Trust Layer upholds data privacy and security across all interactions. Agentforce’s built-in guardrails protect data privacy, security, and accuracy. Testing in a Sandbox validates that these measures work correctly so your organization can confidently deploy secure and compliant AI agents.

Organizations often have unique challenges, such as preventing data loss or monitoring access. Agentforce 360 Platform addresses these needs with solutions like real-time data controls and compliance tools.

5. Fine-tune natural language understanding

Safely test utterances and conversational scenarios to improve  Agentforce’s understanding and response. The Agentforce Testing Center provides a controlled space to simulate real-world interactions. For example, developers can test a range of customer queries and refine how the AI handles edge cases, ensuring reduced misunderstandings and errors when it goes live.

Sometimes, AI can glitch in unexpected ways. Testing helps find unexpected behaviors and quirks that could disrupt user experience or even cause harm.

6. Test and optimize topic engagement

Verify that AI agents correctly interpret and respond to specific topics by testing integrations and configurations. The more you test, the better your agents will understand their skill sets and take more precise actions. Repeated testing with iterations will help confirm accuracy and relevance, boosting performance during real-world interactions.

Enhance your testing with data masking and seeding

While Sandboxes make Agentforce testing less risky, you can take it a step further by quickly populating your sandbox with realistic data using data seeding, especially when a mid-cycle refresh isn’t feasible.

Data masking, on the other hand, is crucial for maintaining data privacy and security. It allows you to use real data for testing without exposing sensitive information. With data masking, you can ensure that while you’re refining your agents, they don’t inadvertently misuse sensitive business or customer PII data. For any organization working with sensitive data, this step is especially important for maintaining compliance and mitigating the risk of data breaches or leaks. It’s even more critical for industries with strict regulatory requirements, such as healthcare and finance. By masking sensitive data, you can simulate real-world scenarios while ensuring compliance and protecting user information.

Prepare your Agentforce for deployment 

Testing lays the groundwork for creating a reliable, high-performing Agentforce that meets expectations immediately. By testing in a Sandbox with realistic, secure data, you can fine-tune your Agentforce in a controlled environment, ensuring seamless performance so you can be sure they’re ready for deployment.

Learn more about risk-free Agentforce testing with Salesforce Sandboxes and Data Mask & Seed.

A match made in innovation

Discover how AI innovations are driving the emergence of agents, and explore how agents are also reshaping software and software development.

Dive in
